meaning: vt.霸占,(对土地、房屋等的)剥夺,使失去,逐出
examples: Pedro could have ensured a bigger margin of victory in the closing stages, but he allowed Arne Friedrich to dispossess him .
And ye shall dispossess the inhabitants of the land, and dwell therein: for I have given you the land to possess it.
Then the LORD will drive out all these nations before you, and you will dispossess nations larger and stronger than you.
Cowie pounced to dispossess David van Zanten after 30 minutes before firing home the opening goal.
Dickson Etuhu appeared to dispossess Youssuf Mulumbu unfairly on the edge of the area but the referee waved play on.
